title: Pass 5.G.3: ferrotorch-serialize sequential shard download / whole-file SHA before stream
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-serialize audit, PERF_OBVIOUS.

- safetensors_io.rs:394-477 / 622-703 — sharded loaders are sequential per shard (no rayon). Single std::thread::spawn exists only in AsyncCheckpointer. For a 70B-model load this is straightforwardly slow.
- safetensors_io.rs:262-280 — bf16/f16 upcast and F32→T narrowing use scalar for loops with per-element cast::<f32, T> + read_unaligned. For Llama-class checkpoints this is the hot path; chunks_exact_iter + f32::from_bits over &[u32] reinterpretation would auto-vectorize.
- gguf.rs:516-800 — eight dequantize_q* routines push values one at a time into Vec::with_capacity; could SIMD via bytemuck.

Bundle adjacent — ferrotorch-hub:
- download.rs:135-148 — whole-file SHA-256 over single Vec<u8> rather than streaming Sha256::update while reading; for 16 GB shards memory pressure + serialized I/O→hash.
- download.rs:560-562 — sequential shard download loop in hub; same shape as safetensors_io.

Acceptance:
- rayon::par_iter over shards in safetensors_io::load_safetensors_sharded
- Stream Sha256::update during download in hub::download_and_verify
- Vectorize bf16/f16 upcast (chunks_exact_iter pattern).
